source_title: Octo: An Open-Source Generalist Robot Policy

ai_summary.one_liner: Open-source generalist robot transformer pre-trained on 800k Open X-Embodiment trajectories with block-wise attention and a diffusion action head for modular fine-tuning to new robots, cameras, and action spaces.
ai_summary.problem: Prior generalist policies (RT-1, RT-2-X) hard-wire the camera, sensor, and action stack into the tokenization and either lose pre-trained weights when migrated to a new robot or ship as closed 55B VLMs that are infeasible to reproduce and extend.
ai_summary.solution: Block-wise causal transformer separating task / observation / readout blocks plus a small diffusion action head; pre-trained on 800k OXE demos with two scales (27M Octo-Small, 93M Octo-Base).
ai_summary.key_result: Outperforms RT-1-X across 9 real robots and 4 institutions, matches RT-2-X (55B) at ~600× smaller, and fine-tunes to new observations / action spaces on a single A5000 GPU in ~5 hours.
limitations: Language conditioning trails goal-image conditioning by ~25% in zero-shot
Heavy reliance on wrist cameras for precise manipulation
93M backbone limits visual/linguistic generalization vs VLM-based successors
No contact/tactile signals at pre-training time
